title: ceratophyllum submersum
intro: Ceratophyllum submersum, also known as the hornwort or coontail, is a type of aquatic plant that belongs to the family Ceratophyllaceae. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and providing antioxidant properties. In herbal preparations, Ceratophyllum submersum is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
